summary: Semantic Search for AI Agents at Scale: Retrieval and Ranking for LinkedIn's Hiring Assistant (15 minute read) LinkedIn built MUSE (Member Understanding Semantic Embeddings) to power semantic search inside Hiring Assistant. It uses a dual-tower Matryoshka embedding model trained on millions of high-quality labels from an LLM Teacher grounded in product policy, combining embedding-based retrieval with a downstream engagement-optimized ranker.

title: Encoding Your Domain Expert: The Context Layer Behind Spotify's Data Assistant
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-15, Reading time: 6 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fengineering.atspotify.com%2F2026%2F6%2Fencoding-your-domain-expert-the-context-layer-behind-spotifys-data-assistant%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019ecac19587-492ddcd8-9693-4a21-b7f1-16bd0b546b72-000000/Mx3if5KkadoPAeabUBy0jmTnHfhsuucjMjUWtO_vZMQ=452
summary: Encoding Your Domain Expert: The Context Layer Behind Spotify's Data Assistant (6 minute read) Spotify built Vedder, an AI data assistant for 2,100+ users across 177 clusters, to move beyond schema-only RAG across 70,000 datasets. Domain experts curate each cluster with datasets, vetted question-SQL pairs, and business docs. Only 12.5% of mined query pairs were accepted, so health scoring tracks drift, validity, coverage, and reproducibility to keep context reliable.

title: How Feldera Works: A True Incremental View Maintenance Engine
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-15, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.junaideffendi.com%2Fp%2Fhow-feldera-works-a-true-incremental%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019ecac19587-492ddcd8-9693-4a21-b7f1-16bd0b546b72-000000/XXzXwyP3PCjJhkxpm4OcPpeuZh0-gorgy2uXYzaM0zg=452
summary: How Feldera Works: A True Incremental View Maintenance Engine (3 minute read) Feldera treats streams as incremental SQL views, using DBSP to propagate deltas instead of recomputing joins and aggregations. Inserts, deletes, and updates become Z-set changes, so only affected rows are updated. The result is batch-SQL-like semantics for continuous pipelines with lower CPU, less memory pressure, and predictable latency.

title: The Mythical Agent-Month
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-15, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwesmckinney.com%2Fblog%2Fmythical-agent-month%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdata/1/0100019ecac19587-492ddcd8-9693-4a21-b7f1-16bd0b546b72-000000/ypO8cG5GVX7O6qkaJZeFmiluW44SK6HL5vH7c8StAJo=452
summary: The Mythical Agent-Month (10 minute read) AI coding agents reduce coding labor, but not the hardest parts of software: design judgment, scope control, testing, and maintainability. They reduce accidental complexity, but can create technical debt, architectural drift, and bloated codebases at machine speed. The edge shifts to experts who can steer the model, say no, and keep systems production-ready.

title: The Bill Arrives: How to Manage Agentic AI Costs at Scale

summary: The Bill Arrives: How to Manage Agentic AI Costs at Scale (17 minute read) Uber's AI budget blowout shows agentic AI is a task-economics problem, not a token-pricing problem. Claude Code adoption hit 84% across 5,000 engineers, exhausting the annual AI budget by mid-April. With spend hidden in re-sent context, retrieval, orchestration, governance, and retries, teams need to measure value per task, control context, and build stateful agent infrastructure.

summary: Apache DataFusion 54.0.0 Released (7 minute read) Apache DataFusion 54.0.0 adds major SQL upgrades, including LATERAL joins, SQL lambda functions for arrays, a new Arrow-based Avro reader, and spill-to-disk for memory-heavy nested loop joins. title: The Log Is the Agent
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-15, Reading time: 11 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2FYNZszp/1/0100019ecad252f4-4a92d1fd-315c-4890-8e44-9b50811ecafe-000000/jMBISGYWSzMBQkzrJ1njAzC6dXrtKoHoEfHPY0TtU0o=452
summary: The Log Is the Agent (11 minute read) While the model, runtime, and loop matter, those things aren't the agent. The agent is the history of events that make up its data. This log allows agents to be reconstructed correctly and resumed. The concept of agents as logs unlocks a whole set of capabilities that make even advanced agent use cases easier to reason about and build into everyday applications.

title: The only scalable delete in Postgres is DROP TABLE
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-15, Reading time: 5 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fplanetscale.com%2Fblog%2Fthe-only-scalable-delete%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ecafb3658-f0f10279-e443-47e3-a2e3-c76e4212e649-000000/hVb2D2O-CMBgyqn5vniD2n3If8Ank54ENxfWPbcreoo=452
summary: The only scalable delete in Postgres is DROP TABLE (5 minute read) Large Postgres DELETEs add work rather than reclaim it. They leave dead tuples, add replication overhead, and don't return disk space, while DROP TABLE and TRUNCATE remove files directly and scale independently of data size. It's best to structure your schema so bulk deletion becomes a DROP or TRUNCATE.

title: Formal methods and the future of programming
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-15, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fblog.janestreet.com%2Fformal-methods-at-jane-street-index%2F%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ecafb3658-f0f10279-e443-47e3-a2e3-c76e4212e649-000000/fRA31GYdkkPajc8-vd0oQznEEmrza4xuqwl0mhgOg3Y=452
summary: Formal methods and the future of programming (10 minute read) Formal methods are techniques that model software as mathematical entities to provide rigorous, proof-based guarantees of correctness rather than relying on standard testing. The rise of agentic coding is now shifting the cost-benefit analysis for these methods, making them an increasingly accessible tool for verifying AI-generated code, whereas before they were too expensive to consistently use.

title: Why AI hasn't replaced software engineers, and won't
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-15, Reading time: 23 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.normaltech.ai%2Fp%2Fwhy-ai-hasnt-replaced-software-engineers%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ecafb3658-f0f10279-e443-47e3-a2e3-c76e4212e649-000000/IKBURK-oNaOlIcIAmtHDWNNjgtscWNMZnuwWsFQN-m8=452
summary: Why AI hasn't replaced software engineers, and won't (23 minute read) AI is unlikely to cause mass layoffs in software engineering because the profession relies on a "decide-execute-deliver" workflow where only the middle execution phase is automated. Recent high-profile job cuts attributed to AI are frequently the result of financial pressures or pandemic-era over-hiring rather than actual technological displacement.

title: Vibe Coder vs Software Engineer
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-15, Reading time: 10 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fyusufaytas.com%2Fvibe-coder-vs-software-engineer%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ecafb3658-f0f10279-e443-47e3-a2e3-c76e4212e649-000000/vShNbJ3Bul2E0sjgaEFrbT39YLhXQu_yFwAqhItTnyo=452
summary: Vibe Coder vs Software Engineer (10 minute read) Vibe coding focuses on the rapid generation of prototypes to test ideas, whereas software engineering prioritizes the entire development lifecycle and the complexities of long-term maintenance. The main difference is in measurement, as the former focuses on the speed of a first working version while the latter values the "time to safe merge.”

summary: Introducing the Open Knowledge Format (10 minute read) The Open Knowledge Format (OKF) is an open specification that formalizes the LLM-wiki pattern into a portable, interoperable format. title: Introducing the Open Knowledge Format

summary: Introducing the Open Knowledge Format (9 minute read) The Open Knowledge Format is an open specification that formalizes the LLM-wiki pattern into a portable, interoperable format. It is vendor-neutral and agent- and human-friendly. The standard can represent the metadata, context, and curated knowledge that modern AI systems need. The specification uses familiar patterns with no complex compression scheme, new runtime, or required SDK.

Unlike Harbor, olmo-eval offers flexibility, minimizing resource use and focusing on development rather than public benchmarking.

title: Stop Monitoring AI Systems Like Web Services
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-16, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.newsletter.swirlai.com%2Fp%2Fstop-monitoring-ai-systems-like-web%3Futm_source=tldrit/1/0100019ed06d0fda-8e5fc794-dd75-4e93-af1d-7547b8386cb4-000000/UCz9Dge54Otja-I6lxZsO3PEvaYfUv3lt7o3PRAd1zk=452
summary: Stop Monitoring AI Systems Like Web Services (8 minute read) LLM-based systems should not be monitored using the same metrics as traditional web services because key failure modes are invisible to uptime, error, and latency dashboards. Monitoring should instead focus on five question groups: speed (latency), scalability (throughput and cost), correctness (quality), reliability, and agent behavior. Many of the required metrics must be built by the AI team rather than emitted by default tooling.

title: Should you post-train your own model?
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-16, Reading time: 4 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Flinks.tldrnewsletter.com%2F9z45Vj/1/0100019ed0991f3b-f67e90d7-f1d3-49c9-b425-0825bd126b69-000000/X9HZmtsotTHRY6ra95pS_K78d4dkMsQl8NtvXgj6DTM=452
summary: Should you post-train your own model? (4 minute read) General frontier models are the right starting point for 0-to-1 prototypes and understanding workflows, but for the handful of power-law use cases critical to a company's mission, product, and margin, the answer is increasingly to post-train your own model. Those use cases are where the differentiated data lives and where hard constraints on cost, latency, and reliability make a general model's single fixed tradeoff a liability.

comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-17, Reading time: 8 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fwww.crunchydata.com%2Fblog%2Fbritish-columbia-and-time-zone-changes%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ed55b202d-e96d02ad-c7f6-45ef-a8f9-23578bd11d5b-000000/S2jKsMb-BSBKLj3CtApr2slSmRzv7orJQWzypqzQ4C0=452
summary: British Columbia, Time Zones, and Postgres (8 minute read) British Columbia's shift to a permanent UTC-7 offset in 2026 shows the risks of storing future appointments in UTC-based database columns, which can lead to unexpected one-hour shifts in local time. These columns convert timestamps based on the rules available at the time of entry, so subsequent updates to time zone data can cause the stored UTC values to no longer match the user's intended wall-clock time.

title: Stop using JWTs
comment: Source: TLDR Newsletter, Date: 2026-06-17, Reading time: 3 minute read, Links: https://tracking.tldrnewsletter.com/CL0/https:%2F%2Fgist.github.com%2Fsamsch%2F0d1f3d3b4745d778f78b230cf6061452%3Futm_source=tldrdev/1/0100019ed55b202d-e96d02ad-c7f6-45ef-a8f9-23578bd11d5b-000000/UBA5iTElWmFVsncjofFcy_6jf4-JjyjggnfduN7n1sg=452
summary: Stop using JWTs (3 minute read) JSON Web Tokens should be avoided for managing user sessions because they are insecure and were never intended for long-term authentication. Traditional cookie sessions are a more reliable alternative, with better flexibility and protection against common security vulnerabilities. Truly stateless authentication is largely impractical, as secure token management usually requires a persistent data store to handle revocations and expiration properly.
